snapshot_metadata.source: CE-PACK-NEUTRAL-DEFAULT — the neutral core-shipped DEFAULT_PACK (harness/pack.py DEFAULT_PACK)
snapshot_metadata.note: The domain-neutral DEFAULT pack: the core boots STANDALONE against this snapshot with no Pro domain pack on disk. It carries the canonical capability-roster IDENTITY the council validates ANY active pack against — council_roster() = the AST-parsed CouncilModel role names UNION the owner roles of pack_tier1_owners(DEFAULT_PACK). So this snapshot's tier1_owners MUST cover the SAME canonical owner-role SET as the former Pro domain default did (risk_judge / policy_judge / faithfulness_judge / behavior_judge / source_message_judge — the last two owner-only, in declared_but_not_running), over NEUTRAL codes, so every existing pack still subset-validates against the canonical roster under the new default. production_judges reuses the role NAMES of the core deployable trio (a pack subsets KNOWN deployable identities; it does not introduce a new deployable judge). tier: core = the OSS default tier. ZERO domain content — this pack must remain domain-agnostic (grep-verifiable: no Pro-domain literal).
